entry.title: Getting authorization error while creating application to sync workflows with SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am following the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-helloworld.html while checking for errors in step 6 I am getting authorization issue while creating the application sync to the workflow . 

Please let me know how can I solve the issue.

Regards

Kaustav

SAP AI Core SAP AI Launchpad

entry.title: Re: Business Network for Logistics - Freight Collaboration: Understanding the Settlement Process
entry.content:
Hello @saurav_das 

I have a very basic question 

1.If the freight order does not have valid freight agreement determined ,in that scenario will the freight order not be visible in the "Invoice Freight Document "app marked with the status "To Be Invoiced."

Cause I am facing some issue with this regard, where the freight order is available in "freight order for confirmation"app but it's not available in "Invoice Freight Document " app as there is not valid agreement determined while charge calculation in SAP TM.

Please help me to understand is freight agreement determination a mandatory pre-requisite for the freight order to be able to invoiced from B4NL FC.

Thanks 

Arjun Mukherjee

entry.title: Re: Schedule Update of Down Payment Status - Sales Orders
entry.content:
Hi there, I doubt something like this exists in on-premise S/4 Cloud. But I would say, try something like this in OP:

 

  1. Create sales order with billing document plan (FAZ - down payment request)
  2. down payment request integrated with FI-CA - The FAZ item creates a request document in FI-CA, assigned to a contract account for the business partner.
  3. Incoming Payment Processed in FI-CA - Payment is posted and clears the FI-CA open item.
  4. Trigger PF Process (SD_ORDER_INTEGRATION) - Process Framework is used to check payment status and update sales order.
  5. Status handling class CL_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD is used to determine whether the payment is sufficient.
  6. Based on the payment, update user status on the SD order (e.g. removing delivery/fact block..).

Use BAdI: IF_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD_BADI: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/021b182b0c47416c8fafed67ebfd78a9/410797ee20e94997813fa62e3d77551e.html

framework Class: CL_CRMS4_IU_CM_PFP_SD (or subclass)

based on payment status in FI-CA, you can conditionally remove billing block in VBAP.

 

Hope this at least gives you some new ideas/thoughts.

 

Best,

Raf

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG-S integration with 3rd party onboarding system
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

Here are the main ways you can integrate a 3rd-party system with SAP MDG (for Suppliers and Materials):

1. Web Services / SOA (Service-Oriented Architecture)

  • MDG provides standard Enterprise Services (ES) that are available via SAP SOA Manager.
  • You can consume MDG services such as:
    • BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest_In (for suppliers)
    • MaterialERPBulkReplicateRequest_In (for materials)
    • BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkCreateRequest_In
  • These services allow create/update/replicate operations.
  • Good option for real-time or near-real-time integration.
  • Can be used by your third-party system to push onboarding data into MDG.

Tip: These services follow SAP's data model, so mapping may be needed between the third-party system’s data and MDG’s structures (like BP, LFM1, LFA1, etc.).

2. IDoc-based Integration

  • Use standard IDocs such as:
    • CREMAS (for supplier master)
    • MATMAS (for material master)
  • This is more batch-oriented but very stable and widely used in legacy scenarios.
  • Can be triggered from SAP PI/PO or even from a flat-file converted to IDoc format.

3. SAP PI/PO or SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)

  • Acts as a middleware layer between the third-party system and MDG.
  • Enables message transformation, routing, and protocol mediation (e.g., HTTPS to SOAP).
  • Preferred if your 3rd-party onboarding tool doesn’t speak SOAP natively or needs additional logic/transformation.
  • You can build custom iFlows for inbound create/change requests or for validations before MDG processing.

4. File-based Import (Staging via Data Import Framework)

  • You can import data into MDG using:
    • MDG Data Import Framework (available in MDG Classic)
    • Staging tables → Data replication
  • This is good for bulk loads (e.g., periodic onboarding from third-party source).
  • You can use:
    • CSV files, Excel files
    • Or custom reports to process the input

5. Custom APIs (OData / REST-based)

  • In case your 3rd-party onboarding tool prefers modern APIs, you can expose:
    • Custom OData Services via Gateway
    • Or build REST adapters in SAP BTP/SAP API Management that wrap MDG's standard SOAP services
  • Best for more flexible integration and newer platforms that don't support SOAP.

6. SAP Master Data Integration (SAP MDI) / Master Data Hub (optional)

  • If you're already using or planning to use SAP MDI as a centralized governance tool across cloud/on-prem systems, it can integrate MDG and your third-party system via a hub approach.
  • However, this adds an additional layer and is relevant mainly in a multi-SAP landscape.

 

I hope this will help you with finding the right fit for your business needs.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: VBEP S/4 HANA VS R/3
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

Let's walk through each of your questions step by step to clarify how VBEP has evolved in SAP S/4HANA compared to R/3, especially with regard to open quantities, new fields, and how it compares to VBBE :

 

1. New Fields in VBEP (S/4HANA)

The fields you mentioned — DLVQTY_BU, DLVQTY_SU, OCDQTY_BU, OCDQTY_SU, ORDQTY_BU, ORDQTY_SU — are part of the data model simplification and enhancement in S/4HANA.

These fields represent quantities in different units of measure (BU = Base Unit, SU = Sales Unit) and are meant to give more granular and real-time visibility into the schedule line status.

Here’s what each means:

Field

Description

DLVQTY_BU

Delivered quantity in Base Unit of Measure (BU)

DLVQTY_SU

Delivered quantity in Sales Unit (SU)

OCDQTY_BU

Open confirmed delivery quantity in Base Unit

OCDQTY_SU

Open confirmed delivery quantity in Sales Unit

ORDQTY_BU

Ordered quantity in Base Unit

ORDQTY_SU

Ordered quantity in Sales Unit

These are redundant fields introduced to improve performance and analytics, avoiding on-the-fly UoM conversions when doing reporting or CDS-based analytics.

These fields are filled in real-time via internal processing (e.g., ATP updates) and are not directly editable via standard transactions.

2. Can I use ODN_AMOUNT to get the open value per schedule line?

Yes — but with some caution.

  • ODN_AMOUNT (Open Delivery Net Amount) is calculated at the schedule line level, unlike some traditional values in VBAP or VBAK.
  • It provides the net amount value (in document currency) corresponding to the open (undelivered) quantity at the schedule line.
  • You can use this field if you want to track open revenue/value for a specific delivery date.

Attention : however, if your pricing includes surcharges, header discounts, or conditions not distributed at the schedule line level, the amount might not match 100% exactly with expectations unless conditions are properly distributed.

3. Is VBEP filtered by open quantity ≠ 0 equivalent to VBBE?

Not quite, although they overlap in use case.

Key differences:

Feature

VBEP with Open Qty ≠ 0

VBBE (Requirements Table)

Source

Schedule line table

ATP (MRP) elements

Includes

All schedule lines (even fulfilled ones if not filtered)

Only open requirements

Use case

Sales schedule details, value tracking

ATP, availability check, MRP planning

Scope

Document-centric

Stock/requirement-centric

So:

  • If you filter VBEP by open quantities (e.g., OCDQTY_BU ≠ 0), you're close to what you get in VBBE, but:
    • VBEP may show more granular info (multiple lines per item/date)
    • VBBE is compiled from VBEP, VBFA, VBKD and other sources during ATP/MRP processes

Best practice : Use VBEP when analyzing document-driven open quantities, and use VBBE for ATP/MRP logic and planning requirements.

 

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud Public without tax engine
entry.content:
Dear user,

And thank you for asking your question on the SAP Community blog.

In S4 S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Brazil, where tax complexity is extremely high and external tax engines (TTEs) are typically required to handle proper tax determination, calculation, and electronic invoicing (NF-e/NFS-e).

Let’s break this down with a clear and practical point of view, especially considering your customer’s unique scenario:

 

Is it technically possible to run S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Brazil without a Tax Engine?

Yes, it is technically possible, but only under very limited and manual conditions.

In your customer's case:

  • Low transaction volume
  • Manual service invoice issuance (via the Prefeitura SP website)
  • No need for automated tax determination or eDocument/NF-e integration
  • Intention to post manually in FI-AR (billing) and FI-AP/MM (invoices/receipts)

So yes, they could operate without a tax engine, but with major limitations and risks.

 

What would you lose or limit by avoiding the tax engine?

Area

What Happens Without a Tax Engine

Tax determination (ICMS/IPI/ISS)

Manual entry in every transaction

NFS-e/NF-e (electronic invoicing)

Not supported without TTE

Compliance with Brazilian tax rules

At risk, no validation against fiscal rules

Real-time integration with SEFAZ/Prefeitura

Unavailable

Fiscal reporting (SPED, GIA, etc.)

May require custom/manual workaround

Mass transaction processing

Not scalable

 

Use Case: Posting Only in FI

If the customer is:

  • Creating and sending service invoices outside SAP (in prefeitura system), and
  • Only posting financial entries manually in SAP (FI-AR and FI-AP), and
  • Not using MM/SD integrated flows for goods or services that require automated tax calculation,

 Then they can operate without a tax engine, because:

  • No automatic tax calculation is needed
  • No eDocument/NF-e generation is required
  • No external service integration is needed

But it's important to:

  • Get clear sign-off from tax and audit teams
  • Understand this setup will not scale well if business grows
  • Accept the risk of non-compliance if users mistype or miscalculate taxes

 

SAP’s Official Position (Best Practice)

SAP strongly recommends a tax engine for Brazil for any productive environment, because:

  • S/4HANA Cloud Public does not include native Brazilian tax logic
  • SAP does not deliver local tax content for Brazil (unlike some on-premise scenarios)
  • Tax engine is required for standard fiscal compliance

 

Recommendation :

For this specific low-volume, high-value service business, a "FI-only, manual entry" approach can work temporarily, as long as:

  • They have clear procedures and internal controls
  • They don’t expect SAP to calculate or determine taxes
  • They don’t require NF-e or NFS-e from within SAP
  • They are OK with not leveraging integrated SD/MM processes

But you should:

  1. Document this approach thoroughly.
  2. Include risks for audit/compliance.
  3. Suggest this only as a temporary or special-case solution.

 

I hope my explanation is clear enough and answer your main questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Connect SAP 4.7 to SAP Netweaver Gateway as a Hub Deployment
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We have requirement to connect SAP 4.7 system with SAP NW gateway system (as a hub deployment). We are planning to expose o-data services through NW gateway system.  But as a first step can we really connect SAP 4.7 with gateway 2.0 as 4.7 doesn't have netweaver components?

If not, then can we have webservice created in SAP 4.7 to expose the data to SAP UI5 applications?

Thank you in advance for your valuable input.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@Sabarim_07 If there's something specific you are having trouble with, let me know. The Mass Maintenance of Service Orders would require a destination to a DMS service and a destination to an S/4HANA system using a user with permission to the Service Orders OData service.

I have been able to get it work -- it is actually pretty cool, using processes, automations, DMS, S/4HANA, Web Call and script activities and more. I will write a blog or do a video on this sometime.

Let me know if I can help you in any way.

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content:
@vishalakshmi You do not have to install the agent for this challenge.

Are you saying that when you click "Go to RBSC Portal" you get the 500 error? What landscape are you on? I'm not really an expert on RBSC, which is not specific to SAP Build Process Automation. It is used to deliver software and check licenses and export controls and perhaps other details. It took me a while to figure out what I needed to do, which I think was more complicated because I am at SAP. For you, you should just need an S-user and a technical user (which I think you create after having an S-user).

https://help.sap.com/docs/RBSC/0a64be17478d4f5ba45d14ab62b0d74c/175673b12feb41739df4f041db52fe76.html?locale=en-US

More info on RBSC in relation to SAP Build Process Automation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/install-desktop-agent-3-and-manage-updates

entry.title: Re: Date Difference is giving validation error in SAP Ana...
entry.content:
MEMBERSET [d/CALMONTH] =[d/Version].[p/START_DATE] TO [d/Version].[p/END_DATE]

MEMBERSET [d/MEASURE]="DEPRECIATION"

MEMBERSET [d/ASSET].[p/START_DATE] != " "

DELETE()

IF DATEDIFF([d/CALMONTH],[d/ASSET].[p/Dreciation_Start_Period],"MONTH")>=0 THEN

DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP([d/CALMONTH]=PREVIOUS(1,"MONTH",[d/Version].[p/Dreciation_Start_Period]),[d/Version]="public.Actual")

ENDIF

But still i am getting the same error.

entry.title: Re: Date Difference is giving validation error in SAP Ana...
entry.content: FYI .. I am getting same error

entry.title: SAP Build showing network issue when calling post call
entry.content:
Hi ,

I was trying my hands at SAP build apps and  I was accessing backend OData call. It gives me a network issue in mobile app. It went well when testing it from integration test and indicated possibility of CORS issue.

I have added "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" in the request and set RZ10 parameter "icf/cors_enabled" as 1. 

Anything else I can do get rid of network issue.

regards,

Gaurang

entry.title: Re: Custom Message Mapping - TPM - B2B - SAP Integration Suite

I've been working for years integrating our thoughts party solution with SAP on customers like Unilever, GSK and Shell. But that was from otherr side of the table.

Recently jumped to SAP side and enjoying the community blogs that give a fantastic landing environment for techies with different experience set.

Let's keep connected.

entry.title: Issue: Form and Configurations Not Saving in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently using SAP Build Apps Free Trial  to carry our an automation project and I’ve been running into an issue where the forms I create — along with their configurations — disappear from the automation page after I log out and log back in.

Here’s what’s happening:

  • I create a form and configure it (fields, logic, bindings, etc.).

  • I make sure to click “Save” before logging out.

  • However, when I log back into SAP Build Apps, the form is either completely missing or all the configurations are gone.

Has anyone else faced this issue?
Any tips on how to make sure changes persist across sessions?
Would appreciate any guidance or best practices around saving and managing form components.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud and Sales&Service Cloud V2 - Difference...
entry.content:
Hi, 

Main filters

The role of main filters is to select objects for replication

Segment filters

You can choose the Show Segment Filters button to create or change filter criteria for segment filters. When you define a segment filter, you ensure that only selected segments of a business objects are replicated. Specifying segment filters reduces not the number of business objects that are replicated, but the size of each business object.

Best regards, Łukasz

entry.title: Additional field on transportation unit (SAP S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have some fields such as temperature temp tale that I want to capture in the transportation unit representing the container. However, I could not find a suitable field on the TU to capture that. There are also no ability to use characteristic fields like what we can do in material master and HU. I looked at use of attributes/attribute groups but that seems to be related to using value list and for categorisation so are not suitable for our purpose. Does anyone know of a better way to incorporate additional fields that are not available in the standard TU?

Appreciate any pointers on the above.

Regards

SFC

entry.title: Are the SF LMS course completion timestamp a trusted\qualified timestamp for the EU funded training?
entry.content: In Italy, but also other EU countries, companies leverage EU funds to get their training reimbursed. In order to benefit from the EU funded training the employers have to report to the funding entities trusted\qualified electronic timestamps. Are the SF LMS course completion timestamps compliant with this legal requirement?

entry.title: Can the internal job posting be restricted to some subset of the workfoce?
entry.content:
Some clients don't want to show to the entire employee population some specific job requisition but only to the employees that have specific jobs, or grad, or department, or location.
While posting in the internal career site, is there any why to give visibility to the Job Requisition to employees based on some rules or attributes\criteria?

Private posting is not a solution for 2 reasons:

1) if target employees forward the link to not target employees they can anyway see the job requisition

2) those internal job market campaigns often target thousands of employees, so recruiters should manage manually massive emails to share the private posting urls.

entry.title: Introduction
entry.content: Hi! I am Kelly and new to this community. I am looking forward to learning more about SAP and the information offered within the community.

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: SCM Optimizer for EWM
entry.content:
With the introduction of Unified Package Building (UPB) in Extended Warehouse Management for SAP S/4HANA, many of our customers would like to understand more about how they could use the SCM Optimizer in their implementation projects and what are the aspects to consider.

We collected some interesting facts and typical questions on SCM Optimizer usage for EWM in the following blog post.

What is the SCM Optimizer?

The SCM Optimizer is a software (implemented in C/C++) that can be installed on the application server or on a separate service running on Windows or Linux. The SCM Optimizer, also called “Optimizer”, can be used by SAP backend solutions, such as Transportation Management or Extended Warehouse Management, for algorithms-based problem solving.

The software component SCM Optimizer contains a collection of different optimizer engines. Each engine is delivered as a separate executable inside the archive. The engines are specialized on different kinds of optimization scenarios, e.g. scheduling or network planning, pallet building and more.

Find more information about the SCM Optimizer via SAP Note 1640509 - FAQ about the setup of SCM Optimizer.

Where is the SCM Optimizer used in EWM for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

Since SAP EWM 9.5, the SCM Optimizer (VSO engine) can be used in EWM through the Unified Package Build, enabling the integration of Package Building (PB) for packing in inbound processes, repacking, outbound delivery processes, as well as posting changes and stock transfer. Learn more about Unified Package Building in EWM and Unified Package Building via SAP Help.

As we gradually introduced new features, you can find an good overview on which process is supported by UPB via SAP Note information 3076865 - Unified Package Building: Additional Application-Specific Information.

While using the Unified Package Building (UPB) is scope of basic functions, the usage of Package Building (PB) in EWM is scope of advanced functions.

When to use the Package Building Optimizer (PBO)?

The advantage of PBO is that it can also build optimized pallets/packages for different sized products considering their dimensions and the weight. You do not need any package instructions or packaging specification, it does a kind of 3D Tetris to build the pallets. There are simple (stacking) rules available, but you can also configure it in more detail. In addition, the results can be visualized in a 3D scene.

How and where to install the SCM Optimizer?

If you are an on-premise customer, you can install the SCM Optimizer on a separate instance (recommended) or on the application service of the consumer solution (e.g. EWM, TM). However, please review SAP Note 1640509 - FAQ about the setup of SCM Optimizer highlighting the advantages and considerations guiding you on this decision.

Please as well review the installation guide mentioned via SAP Note 1686826 - Installation help for installing SCM Optimizers.

If you are a Private Cloud customer, and you have not yet used SCM Optimizer in e.g. your TM PCE system, our Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) team will install the optimizer on your cloud infrastructure. You can contact your local ECS team and create service requests.

Do I have to perform a “SCM Optimizer migration” when I am transforming from Business Suite to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

No, migration activities are not required. The SCM Optimizer is a state-les solution, that does not have an own database or configuration-layer. So, it just can be installed in a greenfield approach during transformation projects. Please ensure that you install the latest optimizer patch available on service marketplace.

As a background, the SCM Optimizer is called from the backend solution with a configuration that is defined in the application layer (e.g. EWM, TM, etc.). This means that there is no migration of SCM Optimizer relevant, when moving from Business Suite (e.g. SAP EWM 9.5) to EWM in SAP S/4HANA (PCE).

What are the commercial aspects to consider using SCM Optimizer in EWM for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE)?

In general the usage of Package Building including Package Building Optimizer in EWM is considered advanced scope (included in the advanced license).

If you are a PCE customer, please note that the infrastructure for the SCM Optimizer is not yet included in the EWM subscription. You could consider adding an additional SAP Connection Package for SCM Optimizer hosting.

In case you already have a subscription for Transportation Management for SAP S/4HANA, etc., the included optimizer ca be used by EWM too.

entry.title: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content:
In the Fiori docu, the scenario of updateing selected rows of a table before updating is described in the docu.

I like to have a toolbar action in the table toolbar in order to preset field values in all rows of a table without the need to select all table rows. Is this supported by Fiori Elements ? 

I tried the following which does not work:

a) definition of my service

service AdminConfigurationService {
  ...
  entity SystemKinds       as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds;
  action   setSystemKindsInitialLanguages();
}

b) service:

class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {

async init() {
  const { SystemKinds } = this.entities;
  ...
   if (!!SystemKinds.texts) {
      this.on('setSystemKindsInitialLanguages', async (req) => {
        let { newTitle, ID } = req.data;

        if (!!SystemKinds.texts?.drafts) {
          await this.update(SystemKinds.texts.drafts, ID).with({ title: newTitle });
        }
      });
    }
    await super.init();
  }
}

c) CustomActionController:

import ExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/core/ExtensionAPI';
import Context from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/Context';
import Dialog from 'sap/m/Dialog';
import Event from 'sap/ui/base/Event';
import Input from 'sap/m/Input';
import Button from 'sap/m/Button';
import ODataModel from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/ODataModel';
import MessageToast from 'sap/m/MessageToast';
import Fragment from 'sap/ui/core/Fragment';

/**
 * Generated event handler.
 *
 * @Param this reference to the 'this' that the event handler is bound to.
 * @Param pageContext the context of the page on which the event was fired
 */

export async function SetTitles(this: ExtensionAPI, pageContext: Context) {
    let fragDialog: Dialog;


    fragDialog = <Dialog>await this.loadFragment({
        id: "paltr_fragmentSystemKindsInitialLanguages",
        name: "paltr.core.configsystemkind.ext.CustomDialogInitialLanguages",
        controller: this
    });

    fragDialog.getBeginButton().attachPress(pressInitialLanguagesSubmit, this);
    fragDialog.getEndButton().attachPress(pressInitialLanguagesCancel, this);

    fragDialog.open();
}

export async function pressInitialLanguagesSubmit(this: ExtensionAPI, pressEvent: Event): Promise<void> {
    const title = (Fragment.byId("paltr_fragmentSystemKindsInitialLanguages", "Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput") as Input).getValue(),
        actionName = "setSystemKindsInitialLanguages",
        context = this.getEditFlow().getView().getBindingContext() as Context;

    let mParameters = {        
        model: this.getEditFlow().getView().getModel() as ODataModel,
        parameterValues: [{
            newTitle: title,            
        }],
        requiresNavigation: false,
        skipParameterDialog: true
    };

    await this.getEditFlow().invokeAction(actionName, mParameters); //SAP Fiori elements EditFlow API

    const dialog = (pressEvent.getSource() as Button).getParent() as Dialog;
    dialog.close();
    dialog.destroy();
    MessageToast.show("Werte gesetzt");
}

export function pressInitialLanguagesCancel(this: ExtensionAPI, pressEvent: Event): void {
    const dialog = (pressEvent.getSource() as Button).getParent() as Dialog;
    dialog.close();
    dialog.destroy();
}

d) fragement:

<core:FragmentDefinition 
xmlns="sap.m" 
xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" 
xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" 
xmlns:macros="sap.fe.macros">
	<Dialog id="paltr_dialogSystemKindsInitialLanguages" title="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesHeader}" class="sapUiResponsivePadding--header sapUiResponsivePadding--content" >
		<HBox alignItems="Center" alignContent="Center">			
			<Label text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesLabel}" class="sapUiSmallMarginEnd" labelFor="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput"/>
			<Input value="{viewModel>/defaulttext}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesInput" maxLength="100" width="30rem"  placeholder="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesPlaceholder}"/> 
		</HBox>
		<beginButton>
			<Button text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesApply}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesButtonApply" />
		</beginButton>
		<endButton>
			<Button text="{i18n>Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesCancel}" id="Paltr_CustomDialogInitialLanguagesButtonCancel"/>
		</endButton>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

Running this, I'm able to press the action button (in my case "Übersetzungen machen") and enter a new title in the dialog popup which should be set to all rows in the table.

If I confirm the dialog with "Anwenden", I will immediately get the error :

(A draft version can only be modified by its associated root entity).
Setting a breakpoint in the service class behind "this.on(....)" will not be reached.
What is my error? How should I implement an action in order to update all rows of a table without selecting the table rows before?

Yours
Stephan

entry.title: Re: How to connect and test CAP application locally with...
entry.content:
Hi,

According to the update in your question and the added screenshots of run configuration, it seems you are not using the recommended Run Configuration option.

I recommend on creating a new Run Configuration for your project, and making sure to select the option marked with "(CAP Node project)". Then you will get much more options than in the screenshot presented, including selecting the authentication mode, OData with live data, etc.

Please note that this option is available in dev spaces of type "Full-Stack Application using Productivity Tools". In case you are using a different dev space type, I suggest to create a new dev space with this type and clone your project to it.

Thanks,

Rotem

entry.title: BDC on t- code EC60
entry.content: Please suggest how to use bdc on ec60 t code as when it comes to meter reading tab there 6 registers readings need to be entered but only 3 registers display at once due to small size of window tab then scroll down is needed to enter left 3 registers readings and bdc may not record scroll down step and therefore reading are not getting put into the registers properly. please suggest

entry.title: Inventory audit report and trail balance amount miss mismatch
entry.content:
Hi All,
There is missmatch for some accounts balance in Inventory Audit Report and Trail Balance , In SAP B1, what can be the possible reason behind this miss match ?

For example as on 31/12/2024

1040080 - Raw Materials - is showing  17,11,57,815.03 amount in Trail Balance and showing  17,17,79,934.85  as per Inventory Audit Report.

Need guidance for investigating it.

entry.title: Re: How to call Function Import of external OData for fetching PDF in CAPM application
entry.content: @Mhsap11 Have you checked whether the request reached the backend? If your backend is S/4HANA on-premise, tracing the request via tr-cd: /iwnfd/traces would help.

entry.title: Re: 360 Evaluation of Competency to PM Form
entry.content: Hello @M_ElHefnawy1 Can i get your contact details i need some assistance on the same thing.

entry.title: Variant Matching doesn't scrap material flagged for deletion
entry.content:
The configured materials even if with obsolete material status (MARA-MSTAE) and with the deletion flag (MARA-LVORM) are not discarded by the variant matching both simulating the configuration (CU50) and in the order entry (VA01)

Before evaluating a massive modification of the obsolete configured codes (thousands) by inserting a "wrong" characteristic value to make the configuration inconsistent, I ask if there is a standard solution or a SAP NOTE to modify the behavior of the system as per examples:

case 1 (two codes configured with the same configuration)
- obsolete code A → discarded by the variant matching
- valid code B → found by the variant matching

case 2 (only one obsolete configured code):
- obsolete code C → discarded by the variant matching (the configurable code remains on the sales order item)

Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Re: Sap Database Fails To Start
entry.content:
After one year using successfully ABAP 7.52 SP04 trial, I get the same error "DB startup failed Exit code 5".

Solution: check and renew the Sybase License. How to do it: Re: ABAP_Trial Installation Problem - SAPSYSTEMNAM... - SAP Community and start the database/ABAP trial again.

entry.title: UI5 App Offline Navigation Issue – Best Approach?
entry.content:
Hey! 
I have developed a UI5 application with two views (main and details). My goal is to use the app in offline mode and still be able to navigate between the views without requiring a network connection.

i tried loading using sap.ui.view and i saw the view and controller loaded, but when navigating offline i still see a fetch trying to get the view even though the files are already fetched! 
Also i tried other ideas but that was the closest i think

Got any ideas? Maybe register the fetched view or connect it to the router so it won’t try to fetch the view? 
thanks!

entry.title: are the email notifications for the influence portal working?
entry.content: I am no longer receiving any emails when influence ideas are posted/updated. Are the emails working correctly at the moment?

entry.title: Add some spice to your life
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about a simple way to add your own flavor when creating a new document in the web client, By leveraging the line type functionality. 

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Re: SAC model calulcation formula editor poping up problem
entry.content:
@Eliram_Lugassi 

Not able to re produce the issue. May be you could try enlarging the code editor and then try writing the formula. Also is it happening only with a specific machine or all users? It could be browser settings, compatibility view etc.

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: AS ABAP 7.52 SP04, Developer Edition: Concise Installation Guide
entry.content:
The VMware guide says to use VMware Workstation Player 14.0 ("Note: Do not install version 15.0 yet. There is currently an issue with version 15 and some versions of Windows").

For information, I have used successfully for one year VMware Workstation Player 17.5.0 with Windows 11 Enterprise 23H2.

VMware Workstation Pro has replaced VMware Workstation Player in 2024, it's free for personal use (VMware Workstation Pro: Now Available Free for Personal Use - VMware Workstation Zealot).

For information, today, I have installed VMware Workstation Pro 17.6.3 (free for personal use), the installation of ABAP 7.52 SP04 was successful, I could login.

entry.title: Re: BDC on t- code EC60
entry.content: BDC is a very old name, it's been named Batch Input for 30 years.

entry.title: Re: Modernizing SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll

entry.title: Re: What is the GS1-128 barcode format
entry.content:
Hi @EmmanuelDeniau ,

I think EAN Code and batch both are should work as split. my assumption is you need enhancement for quantity in that combination. Could you please try below for EAN+batch.

]C1010376006288870710LT102024

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: BDC on t- code EC60
entry.content: There's usually a key or button which does the scroll down, e.g. Enter, Position, Find, etc., if not enhance the code to implement one.

entry.title: Assistance Required for AI Configuration in Business Application Studio [BTP]
entry.content:
Dear BTP Expert Team,

I am new to working on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and have recently integrated it with the Cloud Platform Integration (CPI). I am currently using BTP to resolve tickets for SAP BW and SAP ABAP within the platform.

So far, I have created the global accounts for SAP BW and SAP ABAP, along with sub-accounts for DEV, QM, and Production. I have also logged into the Business Application Studio (BAS) to address the tickets for SAP BW and SAP ABAP.

However, I am facing challenges in configuring AI to assist with ticket resolution in BAS. Could anyone please provide me with the steps or guidance needed to configure AI for this purpose?

entry.title: Re: doubts with table USR40
entry.content:
No, as any other changes to Security Policy Attributes resp. corresponding profile parameters which define password rules, this only takes influence on new passwords but never on existing ones.

Best regards, Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Additional field on transportation unit (SAP S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hi @SFC ,

Standard feature is available to maintain temperature via resource master. Please investigate from your end and let us know if you still need enhancement so someone could provide that.  

 

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: doubts with table USR40
entry.content: Notice: there‘s one Security Policy Attribute which controls whether users will have to change their password if it does not comply with the current password, when logging on by password.

entry.title: Re: Activity backflush with BAPI like MFBF
entry.content: Same problem in 2025, any solution (bapi, fm) for actual activity posting for repetitive manufacturing?

entry.title: Missing steps in "Create and Edit the Automation" Tutorial
entry.content:
I am following the Learning Journey "Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation" (https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/create-processes-and-automations-with-sap-build-process-automation )

Section : Boosting the Sales Order Management Process with an Automation

Exercise: Create and Edit the Automation

Pg. 17, Step 52 of pdf: chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://learnsap.enable-now.cloud.sap/pub/mmcp/project/PR_D1366796546A5FA0/hands_on.pdf

Choose Sales Order > I cannot choose Sales Order even though I have created the Data Type as described in the Tutorial. The new data type is not available as a possible selection.

Data Type exists:

Not available for Selecting as Output Parameter:

I watched the video but it skips this part as well (2:41 of video)

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: Please, colleagues I need your help

Please, share the answer

I think that we need to use :

BAPI_CFG_CREATE

BAPI_CFGINST_CHARCS_VALS_SET

GUID_CREATE

CUCB_CONFIGURATION_TO_DB

CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2

but i can not reproduce CU50

entry.title: Generate IDOC from SAP BTP CAP app
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

For one of the requirements, we need to generate a IDOC in SAP ECC system from SAP BTP CAP application. Can you please give any idea how we can achieve it?

entry.title: Re: AI-Powered Game Changer: Revolutionizing Access to Knowledge
entry.content:
Hi! Thank you for your comments, likes, and thoughts both on and off this landing area! 👏 😊 💡

The goal is to continue the conversation and share SAP Community insights on what we are seeing in respect to the potential for "Revolutionizing Access to Knowledge." Over the last few months (& beyond) I've seen this KM topic first hand from all angles...

We have a saying with another top, global knowledge worker...
"AI may replace workers...but those who understand it and put it to work will have a better chance to be indispensable to their organization either as an employee, worker or consultant."

Stay tuned! More to come! Let me know if you want to have a chat. Happy to bounce a few ideas at an informational level. Perhaps you'd like to see how this Answer Engine works, what it looks like, and how it is helping the SAP Community?

entry.title: Timelines to implement career worksheet & growth portfolio
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a situation where the client is planning internal meetings to evaluate their efforts and allocate resources. To support them and facilitate better planning, I would like to understand the implementation timelines for the career worksheet and growth portfolio, as per the standard process.

I would appreciate it if you could confirm this in the ticket and, if possible, share any high-level timelines, presentation deck, or screenshots.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: That would be easier to enhance CU50 to export the calculated data and batch input CU50.

entry.title: Re: VARIANT CONFIGURATION
entry.content:
Hello,

I try to havé the same behaviour than CU50. Following your post, I used the MF :

BAPI_CFG_CREATE

BAPI_CFGINST_CHARCS_VALS_SET

GUID_CREATE

CUCB_CONFIGURATION_TO_DB

CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2
but I can not reproduce the result of CU50

can you please help me

entry.title: Set restrictions within the Attributes Library for different Legal Entities| Talent Intelligence Hub
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Is it possible to set restrictions within the attributes library? We have a situation where each legal entity is considered a separate client within the global system and would like to have access to their own skills, job catalog, and other resources.

For example, if LE 1 has 10 attributes and LE 2 has 20 attributes, the attributes of these LEs should be accessible or visible to only their users but not to others. This is because they contain data from different clients existing within the same instance. The client wants to enforce restrictions so that this data is only accessible to their own employees.

Any guidance you could provide would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: thanks Sandra_Rossi. i think i m not far from finding the solution. And the result of CU50 have a tree and dynamic tables. So not easy to catch the data i think.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: You need some advice but it's almost done?

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content:
I think you misunderstood what I meant.
I used the folllowing MF:

BAPI_CFG_CREATE

BAPI_CFGINST_CHARCS_VALS_SET

GUID_CREATE

CUCB_CONFIGURATION_TO_DB

CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2

in order to have the same result than CU50. But the table from CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2 return too much lines

entry.title: Re: Unit of Measure in SAP EWM – Materials with ST instea...
entry.content:
Thanks for the help, it worked here.

I just had to post goods receipt for a new stock, so it changed to the right AUoM (the suggestion to use /scwm/post also works). 
Basically:

  • Define Unit of Measure Attributes -> mark AUOM as specific unit of measure
  • Define Item Types for Outbound Delivery Process -> set the flag to keep the AUoM from delivery of ERP

entry.title: Re: Supplier confirmation S/4 publice cloud
entry.content:
Hello Amir

CH means Chang Confirmation. This confirmation is used when the supplier makes changes to the original order, such as changes to the delivery quantity or delivery date.

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: How does config change to payroll ID auto-generation impacts reference code creation & sync?
entry.content:
With onboarding 2.0 payroll ID generation is configured as part of the process. The configuration is and was set to Yes to auto-generate payroll ID. The reference code has to be unique to ensure integrated payroll system picks up the data accordingly and create a payroll profile.

How does the payroll ID generation relates to the 'reference code' creation?

Would the payroll ID auto-generation switch to No (from default Yes) impact the reference code generation? Could it result in duplicate reference code creations?

What are the possible downstream impacts we need to consider before initiating such change in the settings?

@dayforce-SAP SuccessFactors

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Link Production Order to SAP CPM Change Request
entry.content: You will need to do custom development to bring in an account assigned production order as an object in CPM.  This has been done on customer CPM implementations.

entry.title: Re: Intercompany Billing without Sales Order or STO
entry.content:
Refer to this section on SAP Help to set up Intercompany RRB.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4dd8cb7b1c484b4b93af84d00f60fdb8/bcc5759370a045f69d4843eb6150b5c2.html?q=intercompany+project

entry.title: SAP Inside Track Campinas: knowledge, innovation, and community in action!
entry.content:
For those who don’t know yet, SAP Inside Track (SIT) events are independent community-driven gatherings, organized by SAP professionals to share practical knowledge, connect people, and promote innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

This year, the SAP Brazil community is on the move, with events confirmed in Rio de Janeiro, Campinas, Recife, Fortaleza, São Paulo, Salvador, and SIT Cerrado — a true knowledge tour across the country!

🌍And it doesn't stop there: in Portugal, the SAP Inside Track will take place on the last Saturday of September at The Lodge Hotel in Porto. A must-attend for anyone in the European SAP scene!

🔍What happened at SIT Campinas?

The event was packed with high-level content, with a strong focus on current and relevant topics:

🧠 Generative AI and AI Agents: Sessions explored how these technologies are being applied in the SAP landscape.
🤖Joule, SAP’s AI assistant: The intelligent assistant embedded into SAP Business Technology Platform was a hot topic throughout the day.
📊Tax Reform in Brazil: A pressing matter — we have until the end of 2025 to prepare SAP systems for the new tax model that takes effect on January 1st, 2026. A massive challenge for both tax and ERP teams.

🚀During my presentation, I shared how bank connectivity in SAP can be implemented using banking APIs, improving automation and security in financial processes.

And of course, keeping in line with the theme of the moment, I also introduced how Makevalue’s AI can be applied to Cash Flow Planning, showing how artificial intelligence can transform financial management.

👏Congratulations to all the amazing speakers — João Secco, Fabio Dezan, Thamires Kuwahara, Arthur Placido, Rodrigo Ramos Cordeiro, Luciano Casado da Silva Filho — and a big thank you to the organizing team and volunteers, especially Roberta Fernandes , who made this event possible.

 

🌟See you all soon at SAP Inside Track Salvador!

entry.title: Re: No JSON payload template for event situations to crea...
entry.content:
Hi @SAPSupport ,

 

if this answer comes from AI agent, this would be embarrassing, Sorry!
Maybe I´m wrong, then correct me.

Compared to SAP Solution Manager, SAP Cloud ALM does not come with an embedded ITSM. Which is fair enough.
It will support external ITSM with providing interfaces.

ServiceNow and Atlassian (Jira Service Desk) are leader in their market (Gartner).
Customers don't expect a wide range of supported third-party solutions but the leaders should be included!
For SAP it is easy (low hang fruit) to provide this JSON mapping for JIRA standard incident (ITIL).
We as customers, expect those integration aspects.
The Customer Influencing Program is for improvement potential and not for missing standard functions.
As far as I can remember, SAP`s last strategic mission was integration.

Providing this kind of answer, referring to standard notes and the help portal is not worth providing and reading.
Sorry, this is just wasting customers' time & make them upset.
For sure we know all these sources.

If you don't have valuable input, please don't provide any.

Regards

Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP Solution unit onboarding guide for e-Invoicing in KSA
entry.content:
Dear Zaheer,

Need 1 input. We had gone live with ERP on promise ZATCA integration using CPI. It is working fine with both QA and PRD connected to ZATCA using 2 different tenants of CPI ( QA and PRD respectively).

As part of the system refresh, our ERP QA system is copied from the ERP PRD system.

We changed the EQA endpoint to CPI QA endpoint after system copy, but now QA ZATCA integration is failing with a credential issue.

Any idea how to resolve the QA issue? ERP PRD, we have not touched it and it is working fine.

Thanks in advance.
